meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Solve Project Abandonment
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Learn how a lean analytics setup using Antigravity and Claude Code helps founders validate ideas and ship faster. Beginner guide.
content.answer_block: Most founders quit because they lack visibility into user behavior, leading to 'feature creep' instead of product-market fit. By implementing a lightweight analytics layer early, you gain data-driven confidence. This setup uses Antigravity for rapid architecture and Claude Code for automated implementation, turning guesswork into actionable growth metrics.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a half-finished dashboard, the excitement of launch day replaced by the crushing weight of feature bloat. You haven't failed because of your code; you’ve failed because you’re building in a vacuum. Without data, every line you write is a gamble, and the uncertainty is exactly what kills your momentum.

But what if you could trade that anxiety for cold, hard data? By integrating a high-signal analytics layer into your MVP, you stop guessing what users want and start measuring how they actually behave. This isn't just about tracking clicks—it's about building a feedback loop that validates your business model in real-time, keeping you focused on the features that actually drive revenue.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the one action that proves your product provides value. This prevents scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my SaaS business model and identify the single most important user event to track for early-stage validation. Provide a schema for a minimal tracking event payload.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a tracking_events.ts file defining the event interface for my core user action.'

steps.title: Architecting the Analytics Provider
steps.description: Build a decoupled analytics wrapper so you can swap providers later without rewriting your entire codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular AnalyticsProvider class in TypeScript that supports event logging and user identification with a provider-agnostic interface.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the AnalyticsProvider class in src/services/analytics.ts and export a singleton instance.'

steps.title: Instrumenting High-Value User Flows
steps.description: Inject tracking into the 'Happy Path' of your application to see where users drop off during onboarding.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware or hook implementation that automatically triggers a 'conversion_event' when a user completes the signup process.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add the analytics.track('signup_complete') call to the user registration component.'

steps.title: Automating Session Persistence
steps.description: Ensure user sessions are consistent across page refreshes to get accurate funnel data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a utility to persist anonymous user IDs in local storage and associate them with identified users upon login.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the authentication flow to identify the user in the analytics service immediately after login.'

steps.title: Validation and Debugging
steps.description: Verify your events are firing correctly before deploying to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a test script that simulates user navigation and verifies that analytics events are correctly captured and logged to the console.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a test file in __tests__/analytics.test.ts to verify event dispatching.'

pro_tips: Track 'intent' events (e.g., clicking a pricing button) rather than just 'success' events to measure demand before features are built.
Keep your analytics payload under 1KB to ensure zero impact on your Core Web Vitals.
Use a single global analytics singleton to prevent event duplication across your component tree.
Always include a 'debug mode' toggle in your production environment that logs events to the browser console for instant QA.
Don't track everything; focus on the 20% of user actions that generate 80% of your revenue.
content.next_steps: Now that you have telemetry, deploy your MVP to a beta group of 10 users. Watch the analytics dashboard for 48 hours. If they aren't hitting your North Star metric, pivot your feature set before spending another week on development.
content.conversion_block: Stop guessing and start shipping products that people actually use. By mastering your data, you eliminate the uncertainty that causes most founders to stall. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
